what_it_does: Implements the Company inward-facing noticeboard runtime: filing, reading, listing, transitioning, editing, and relating typed board items stored as flat frontmatter markdown files under channel-memory/noticeboard/<id>.md, with registry-driven item types, sources, edge kinds, and lifecycles.
observations: All type/source/edge references are validated against lazy-loaded file-discovered registries (item_types/, source_types/, board_edges/).
Items are stored flat by id; the canonical address is board://<id>.
Frontmatter holds the structured row; body is markdown after the fence.
Lifecycle transitions are declared on the item-type row and enforced by transition().
Typed edges in links are validated against the RelationTypeRegistry.
traverse() resolves edge targets through runtime.cognition.resolve_address.
reverse_traverse() finds items linking to a target address by opaque string match.
relations() unifies outgoing and incoming edge queries.
comment()/reply()/thread() provide addressable annotation threading on any address.
assemble_document() reads a document plus ordered blocks, each with its own thread.
standards: The relations() function is the central typed-edge query surface combining out-edges and in-edges.
traverse() and reverse_traverse() compose the board with the single cognition.resolve_address resolver.
Comments are first-class board items linked to any address, enabling recursive reply threading.
Document assembly composes ordered blocks with their threaded comments.
conventions: Fail-loud: every bad registry reference, illegal transition, or missing item raises BoardError.
Lazy module-level singleton registries with reset_registries() to prove add-a-row-is-live.
Reuse-don't-parallel: frontmatter parsing and durable writes delegated to existing modules.
Extensive docstrings state design laws and cross-file relationships.
concerns: list_items() silently skips malformed records via a broad except BoardError, which weakens the file's otherwise fail-loud stance.
_split_frontmatter partially re-parses the frontmatter boundary even though _fm_extract already extracts metadata.
edit_item() records a history entry with from:'edit', which is not a real state and may confuse lifecycle consumers.
traverse() passes store=None to resolve_address by default, which may raise if the resolver requires a live store.
No concurrency control for flat-file writes.
notes: This is the registry-backed, flat-file noticeboard heart of the Company, with typed edges and threaded annotations.
questions: What exact interface do ItemTypeRegistry, SourceTypeRegistry, and RelationTypeRegistry expose (initial, legal_from, etc.)?
How does runtime.cognition.resolve_address handle board://, session://, skill:// targets and what does it require from store?
Are item types such as 'note' actually registered, given comment()/reply() default to item_type='note'?
How is the channel/thread field intended to be populated by the outward channel system?
What is the intended behavior when an address in a document's order no longer resolves?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file builds an inward-facing ledger of typed items with registry-driven lifecycles, typed edges, and annotation threading, exactly as the module docstring describes.
intention_signals: The file is trying to create a durable, addressable, self-describing noticeboard where the Company can file requests, issues, tips, guides, and ideas, move them through registry-defined lifecycles, link them to any other addressed resource, and hold threaded conversations on any address.
novelty: The board edge layer is treated as a cross-registry relation vocabulary, with a single relations() query surface that works structurally and optionally hydrates through the one address resolver; annotation threading is first-class rather than tool-specific.
contribution: Provides the noticeboard runtime: registry-backed item types/sources/edges, flat frontmatter storage, lifecycle transitions, typed edge traversal and reverse traversal, a unified relations query, comment/reply threading, and document assembly with threaded blocks.
summary_for_embedding: A registry-driven noticeboard runtime that files, reads, transitions, and relates typed board items at board://<id> using flat frontmatter markdown files, with typed cross-address edges and threaded annotations.
intention_for_embedding: To give the Company an inward-facing, addressable ledger for self-referential items with registry-defined lifecycles, typed edges to other addressed resources, and recursive comment threading.

symbols.does: Exception class for board operation failures.
symbols.description: A RuntimeError subclass raised whenever a board operation encounters an unknown registry reference, illegal transition, missing item, or malformed input; the module promises never to silently no-op.

symbols.does: Lazy singleton accessor for the item-type registry.
symbols.description: Returns a cached ItemTypeRegistry discovered from item_types/; creates it on first call and reuses it until reset_registries() clears it.

symbols.does: Lazy singleton accessor for the source-type registry.
symbols.description: Returns a cached SourceTypeRegistry discovered from source_types/; shared module-level singleton.

symbols.does: Lazy singleton accessor for the board edge-kind registry.
symbols.description: Returns a cached RelationTypeRegistry discovered from board_edges/; provides the vocabulary for typed item links.

symbols.does: Clears and reloads all three lazy registries.
symbols.description: Drops the cached item, source, and edge registries and immediately re-discovers them, proving that adding a new row file becomes live without code changes.

symbols.does: Returns sorted registered item-type ids.
symbols.description: A small convenience projection over the item-type registry, used for error messages and validation.

symbols.does: Returns sorted registered source-type ids.
symbols.description: A small convenience projection over the source-type registry, used for validation in file_item.

symbols.does: Returns sorted registered board edge-kind ids.
symbols.description: A small convenience projection over the edge-kind registry, used for link validation and error messages.

symbols.does: Returns the registry-declared initial state for an item type.
symbols.description: Looks up the item type in the registry and returns its initial lifecycle state, raising BoardError if the type is unknown.

symbols.does: Returns states legally reachable from a given state for an item type.
symbols.description: Consults the item-type row's lifecycle to list allowed next states from the current state, failing loud on unknown type.

symbols.does: Returns the current UTC timestamp as an ISO string.
symbols.description: Simple helper used to stamp created, updated, and history entries consistently.

symbols.does: Generates a short random board item id.
symbols.description: Creates an 'item-' prefix followed by eight hex characters from a UUID; used when filing new items.

symbols.does: Resolves the effective noticeboard directory.
symbols.description: Returns the provided board_dir or falls back to the default channel-memory/noticeboard path.

symbols.does: Builds the filesystem path for a given item id.
symbols.description: Joins the effective board directory with '<id>.md' to locate the flat storage file.

symbols.does: Validates a list of typed edge links.
symbols.description: Ensures each link is a dict with kind and target, and that kind exists in the edge registry; returns normalized links or raises BoardError.

symbols.does: Serializes a record to frontmatter markdown.
symbols.description: Writes the structured fields as YAML inside '---' fences followed by the markdown body, preserving the body as free text.

symbols.does: Parses a stored record into metadata and body.
symbols.description: Uses lifters.frontmatter._extract for metadata and manually extracts everything after the closing fence as the body.

symbols.does: Atomically persists a record to its flat file.
symbols.description: Creates the board directory if needed and calls store.fs_store._atomic_write_fsync for durable, crash-safe writes.

symbols.does: Files a new typed item onto the board.
symbols.description: Validates type, source, and links against their registries, assigns a new id and board:// address, sets the initial state, records provenance and history, and persists the item.

symbols.does: Reads one item by id, failing loud if missing or unparseable.
symbols.description: Loads the .md file, splits frontmatter and body, ensures an id is present, and returns the full record including links and history defaults.

symbols.does: Lists board items with optional filters.
symbols.description: Scans the flat directory, reads each .md item, and filters by type, state, source, or author_session; silently skips malformed records.

symbols.does: Follows a board item's outgoing typed edges through the resolver.
symbols.description: Reads the item's links, optionally filters by kind, and resolves each target via runtime.cognition.resolve_address, returning one hop per edge with the resolved result.

symbols.does: Finds all board items that link to a target address.
symbols.description: Scans the board for links whose target string equals the given address, optionally filtered by kind; with hydrate=True it also resolves each source address through the resolver.

symbols.does: Structural read of a board item's outgoing edges.
symbols.description: Returns the item's links optionally filtered by kind, without resolving targets unless hydrate=True; used as the building block for relations().

symbols.does: Unified typed-edge query surface for any address.
symbols.description: Returns incoming and/or outgoing edges for an address: outgoing only for board:// items, incoming for any address; supports kind filtering and optional hydration through the resolver.

symbols.does: Moves an item along its registry-declared lifecycle.
symbols.description: Validates that the requested state is legal from the current state for the item's type, updates state and updated timestamp, appends a history entry, and persists.

symbols.does: Edits an item in place without changing its address.
symbols.description: Updates title, body, order, or appends links, validates new links, stamps updated, appends an edited history entry, and persists the same file.

symbols.does: Files a comment item linked to any address.
symbols.description: Creates a board item with a commented_on edge pointing at target_addr, making the comment itself addressable and replyable.

symbols.does: Files a reply item linked to a comment address.
symbols.description: Creates a board item with a reply_to edge pointing at comment_addr, supporting nested threading.

symbols.does: Builds the threaded annotation tree on an address.
symbols.description: Returns top-level comments linked via commented_on to addr, each nested with recursive replies linked via reply_to, by reusing reverse_traverse.

symbols.does: Recursively collects replies to a comment address.
symbols.description: Inner helper of thread() that returns reply items linked reply_to to the given address, recursively nesting their own replies.

symbols.does: Reads a document as an ordered, annotated whole.
symbols.description: Fetches the document record, resolves its ordered blocks (from order or part_of fallback), includes each block's body and threaded comments, and also includes the document-level thread and any missing block addresses.
